 {"id":"frankenredis-icsm","title":"EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking","description":"# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic. The benchmark harness must exist before any optimization work can be proven.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:22.979891472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057699153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057479858Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: benchmark harness (bxlo), baselines (gess), regression gate (1zxx) all closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1zxx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.512824074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-bxlo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.348066219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gess","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.429198141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"frankenredis-iniy","title":"Add bitfield and BITOP fuzz target for fr-store binary string mutations","description":"Evidence: there is no direct fuzz target for fr-store bit mutation surfaces even though they perform offset arithmetic and dynamic buffer growth. The uncovered entry points are crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 (bitfield_get), 2320 (bitfield_set), 8000 (bitop), plus internal helpers 10206 (bitfield_read) and 10235 (bitfield_write). Existing fuzz_runtime_sequence does not drive these paths.\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that mutates offsets, signed/unsigned widths, BITOP opcodes, and source/destination key combinations, then checks invariants against a small shadow bit-vector model: reads after writes agree, no-op writes preserve digest, BITOP output length matches Redis rules, and hostile offsets never panic or corrupt unrelated keys.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs calling the fr-store bitfield/bitop APIs directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-store","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-irk8","title":"EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in","description":"# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.896335079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.672075914Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.671896515Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-06pn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.436452385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-42df","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.828280032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-6axy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.963579323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-e1e9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.699590804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-v7zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.569119622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ix1","title":"Normalize sorted-set zero scores to avoid -0 regressions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:27:04.284868059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.689735969Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.687284050Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-iz1j","title":"Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync","description":"# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:15:36.338674922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.005179144Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.004893794Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.676226414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"frankenredis-oe10","title":"Runtime does not yet consume Lua script propagation masks","description":"Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs and crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs now record per-command Lua propagation masks for redis.set_repl(REPL_NONE/REPL_AOF/REPL_REPLICA/REPL_ALL), but fr-runtime still records top-level EVAL/FCALL writes without consulting that metadata.

Gap: selective script propagation is now modeled in command/store state, but runtime backlog/AOF capture still needs to consume those records so replica backlog and script-side propagation control diverge less from Redis.

Real impl path: thread store.script_propagation_records through the runtime EVAL/FCALL capture path in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs and decide how to route AOF-vs-replica destinations against the existing persistence/backlog model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:22:29.484498154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:50:10.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:50:10.000000000Z","close_reason":"Runtime now consumes Lua script propagation records","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-oe10","title":"Runtime does not yet consume Lua script propagation masks","description":"Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs and crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs now record per-command Lua propagation masks for redis.set_repl(REPL_NONE/REPL_AOF/REPL_REPLICA/REPL_ALL), but fr-runtime still records top-level EVAL/FCALL writes without consulting that metadata.

Gap: selective script propagation is now modeled in command/store state, but runtime backlog/AOF capture still needs to consume those records so replica backlog and script-side propagation control diverge less from Redis.

Real impl path: thread store.script_propagation_records through the runtime EVAL/FCALL capture path in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs and decide how to route AOF-vs-replica destinations against the existing persistence/backlog model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:22:29.484498154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:50:10Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:50:10Z","close_reason":"Runtime now consumes Lua script propagation records","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-slex","title":"Add stateful fuzz target for fr-store streams and consumer groups","description":"Evidence: existing fuzz_runtime_sequence only covers basic string/list/set/hash/zset/key/db commands in fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_runtime_sequence.rs, but it never exercises stream or consumer-group state. High-risk uncovered entry points live in crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:6609 (xadd), 6873 (xread), 6906 (xreadgroup), 7367 (xgroup_create), 7412 (xgroup_destroy), 7443 (xgroup_setid), 7505 (xgroup_createconsumer), and 7584 (xgroup_delconsumer).\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware/stateful libFuzzer target under fuzz/fuzz_targets/ that drives stream IDs, pending entries, consumer creation/deletion, and readgroup ack-like progress through a shadow model or invariant suite (no duplicate delivery regressions, pending counts stay nonnegative, failed ops do not mutate unrelated state, dump/restore preserves stream-group metadata).\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_stream_groups.rs targeting the fr-store stream/group APIs directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:47.378853544Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:47.378853544Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-store","fuzz","testing"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-st3p","title":"Add semantic fuzz target for fr-store PFADD/PFCOUNT/PFMERGE invariants","description":"Evidence: HyperLogLog operations are implemented in crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:7687 (pfadd), 7736 (pfcount), and 7758 (pfmerge), with only unit tests today. No dedicated fuzz harness checks hostile register payloads, merge idempotence, or monotonic count expectations.\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that drives multiple HLL keys through PFADD/PFCOUNT/PFMERGE sequences and asserts semantic invariants: repeated adds do not shrink counts, merges are idempotent, merging identical sources is stable, type errors do not mutate existing HLLs, and dump/restore preserves approximate counts within deterministic self-roundtrip bounds.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_hll.rs hitting the fr-store PF* APIs directly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:34.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:34.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-store","fuzz","testing"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-su4k","title":"Add structured parser fuzz target for fr-command stream and option parsers","description":"Evidence: existing command fuzzing covers classification, frame round-trip, MIGRATE, CLIENT TRACKING, and CLIENT REPLY, but several internal option parsers are still only unit-tested. High-value uncovered entry points include crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:3308 (parse_zrangebyscore_opts), 3666 (parse_geo_search_flags), 4262 (parse_stream_id), 4314 (parse_stream_range_bound), 4345 (parse_xread_id), 8748 (parse_zstore_args), 11765 (parse_scan_args), 12960 (parse_eval_args), and 14190 (parse_blocking_timeout).\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that generates argv slices for these parser families, compares accepted outputs against normalization invariants, and asserts rejected inputs fail without panicking. For stream IDs and blocking timeouts, include boundary and canonicalization checks; for zstore/scan/geo parsers, verify option order and duplicate-option handling are stable.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_command_option_parsers.rs against fr-command parser helpers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:34.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:34.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-command","fuzz","testing"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-sv9n","title":"Add scan-family fuzz target for fr-store cursor and pattern iteration","description":"Evidence: fr-store exposes cursor-driven iterators at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:8421 (scan), 8471 (hscan), 8522 (sscan), and 8572 (zscan), but current fuzz coverage only hits glob matching and basic runtime sequences. There is no harness asserting cursor progress, duplicate suppression, or MATCH/COUNT stability across repeated scans.\n\nNeed: add a stateful/structure-aware fuzz target that seeds mixed keyspaces and collection contents, drives repeated SCAN/HSCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN calls with fuzzed cursor/pattern/count inputs, and checks invariants: cursors eventually terminate, concatenated results are subsets of the live container, no item appears outside the selected type, and MATCH filtering agrees with the existing glob matcher.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_scan_family.rs targeting the fr-store scan APIs directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:34.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:34.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-store","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-slh","title":"AOF persistence integration (load on startup, fsync on write)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-18T05:53:30.760695013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:51.832350476Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:51.831686530Z","close_reason":"Added --aof CLI flag, AOF load on startup, persistence wired through existing Runtime.set_aof_path/load_aof","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-slh","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-qtv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T05:53:49.240874957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-smfv","title":"[MOCK] Lua redis.set_repl is still a no-op stub","description":"Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2483 returns nil with comment "No-op: replication control stub".

Current gap: Lua scripts can call redis.set_repl, but the evaluator drops the request instead of changing replication behavior, so the scripting surface still contains a placeholder API.

Real impl path: implement replication-mode state in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs near the redis.replicate_commands/redis.set_repl dispatch, then thread that state through the script execution path in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs and downstream propagation in crates/fr-repl.","notes":"Blocked: correct REPL_NONE/REPL_AOF/REPL_REPLICA/REPL_ALL semantics require runtime-visible per-script propagation; FrankenRedis currently uses one shared top-level command stream for both persistence and replication, so this cannot be landed as a local stub cleanup.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:43:03.066080367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:22:16.869267435Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:22:16.867489971Z","close_reason":"Removed literal redis.set_repl no-op stub and now track per-command propagation masks in Lua/store state","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-sn2","title":"Reconcile spec corpus evidence pointers with checked-in artifacts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:28:26.093220159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:29:17.811330855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:29:17.811067306Z","close_reason":"Reconciled spec corpus evidence pointers with checked-in artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
